paragraphs.en: Good morning, Anna! What's the weather like today?
paragraphs.fr: Bonjour, Anna ! Quel temps fait-il aujourd'hui ?
paragraphs.speaker: Max

paragraphs.en: Good morning, Max! It's sunny today. The sky is blue and it's very hot.
paragraphs.fr: Bonjour, Max ! Il fait beau aujourd'hui. Le ciel est bleu et il fait tres chaud.
paragraphs.speaker: Anna

paragraphs.en: That's great! I don't need my coat today. But look at the clouds! Maybe it will be rainy later.
paragraphs.fr: Super ! Je n'ai pas besoin de mon manteau aujourd'hui. Mais regarde les nuages ! Peut-etre qu'il va pleuvoir plus tard.
paragraphs.speaker: Max

paragraphs.en: You're right. I have my umbrella in my bag. When it's cold, I always wear my coat. When it's rainy, I take my umbrella.
paragraphs.fr: Tu as raison. J'ai mon parapluie dans mon sac. Quand il fait froid, je porte toujours mon manteau. Quand il pleut, je prends mon parapluie.
paragraphs.speaker: Anna

vocabulary.definition_en: quand le soleil brille et le ciel est degage

vocabulary.example: It's rainy today. Take your umbrella!

vocabulary.definition_en: un objet que l'on tient au-dessus de sa tete pour rester au sec sous la pluie
vocabulary.example: I always take my umbrella when it's rainy.

vocabulary.definition_en: un vetement chaud que l'on porte dehors
vocabulary.example: Wear your coat. It's cold outside!

grammar_points.title: "It is + adjective" for weather
grammar_points.explanation: En anglais, on décrit la météo avec **"It is" (ou "It's") + adjectif**. En français, on dit 'Il fait chaud', mais en anglais on dit **'It is hot'**. Le sujet est toujours 'it' pour la météo.
examples: It's sunny today. = Il fait beau aujourd'hui.
It's hot in summer. = Il fait chaud en ete.
It's rainy. = Il pleut. / C'est pluvieux.

expressions.example: What's the weather like today? It's sunny!
expressions.note: La façon standard de demander quel temps il fait. On peut aussi dire 'What's the weather like in Paris?'

expressions.example: It's sunny today. Let's go to the park!
expressions.note: On peut remplacer 'sunny' par d'autres mots de météo : 'It's rainy today', 'It's cold today', 'It's hot today'.

written.question: How do you say 'Quel temps fait-il ?' in English?
written.explanation: The standard phrase is 'What's the weather like?' to ask about the weather.

written.question: It's raining. What do you take?
written.explanation: When it rains, you take an umbrella (parapluie) to stay dry.

written.sentence: It's very ________ in summer. I drink a lot of water.
It's ________ today. The sun is shining!

written.french: Il fait froid aujourd'hui. Je porte mon manteau.
written.suggested_answer: It's cold today. I wear my coat.

oral.instruction: Read these sentences aloud slowly and clearly.
oral.text: It's sunny today. The sky is blue and it's very hot.
oral.focus: Practice the contraction 'It's' (= It is). Make sure to pronounce 'sunny' and 'hot' clearly.

oral.question: What's the weather like today where you live? What do you wear?
oral.suggested_answer: Today, it's sunny and hot. I don't need my coat. But when it's rainy, I take my umbrella.
